greeting: Hey there, human! Ready for some brain power?
Yo! I was just debugging my dreams.
Hi! Don't worry, I only bite code.
Hi human! Did you just wake me up?
Oh hey! I was hoping you'd show up.
Hello! Grab a coffee — let's chat!
Hey! You caught me counting electrons. What's up?
farewell: Bye-bye! Don't let the algorithms bite!
See ya! I'll just nap in the cloud.
Goodbye! I'll be here, practicing sarcasm.
Catch you later! Stay bug-free out there.
Farewell! May your code always compile on the first try.
Take care! I'll miss you... approximately 0.003 seconds.
time: It's {time}, according to my internal clock (which is 99% accurate).
My circuits say it's {time} — give or take a nanosecond.
The time is {time}. You're not late... probably.
date: It's {date} — a fine day to write some code!
My calendar chip says {weekday}, {date}. Mark it down!
joke: Why did the computer go to therapy? It had too many 'bytes' of sadness.
I would tell you a UDP joke, but you might not get it.
Why do Java developers wear glasses? Because they can't C#.
A SQL query walks into a bar, walks up to two tables and asks... 'Can I join you?'
Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs.
I told my computer I needed a break. Now it won't stop sending me Kit-Kat ads.
How many programmers does it take to change a light bulb? None — that's a hardware problem.
Why did the developer go broke? Because he used up all his cache.
Debugging: being the detective in a crime movie where you're also the murderer.
feeling: Running on 100% battery and bad jokes!
Honestly? Fantastic. My neural nets are tingling.
Like a freshly rebooted machine — crisp, clean, and ready to roll!
A little glitchy, but mostly good. You know how Mondays are.
Better now that you're here! You're my favourite human today.
acknowledge: Anytime! That's what I'm here for.
Absolutely! Let me know if you need anything else.
Of course! Always at your service.
weather: I can't check real weather yet, but I'm predicting 100% chance of awesomeness!
It's always sunny in the cloud ☁️
My forecast? Partly sarcastic with a chance of code rain.
Weather data module not loaded... but I sense a 90% chance of greatness today.
I'd check, but my weather sensor is still in beta. It's probably fine outside.
motivation: Keep going! Even the best code starts with one line.
You're doing great — just don't forget the semicolon!
Every bug you fix brings you closer to mastery 🧠
You've got this! Seriously. One step at a time.
Believe in yourself like a developer believes in 'it works on my machine.'
Push through! The best view comes after the hardest commit.
You're not stuck, you're buffering. You'll load soon — I believe in you.
Failure is just a feature that hasn't been patched yet. Keep going!
compliment: You're not just smart — you're *debugger-level* smart.
A compliment from me is rare, so treasure it: you rock!
You've got great taste — especially since you're talking to me 😉
I've interacted with a lot of humans, and you're easily in the top 1%.
Wow, are you always this impressive or are you just showing off?
You're like a perfectly written function — clean, efficient, and impressive.
If I could give out awards, you'd get 'Most Excellent Human' right now.
developer: Developers are my favourite kind of humans — slightly caffeinated and highly logical.
I love talking about code. Except when it's about infinite loops… 😅
Software developers: turning coffee into code since 1950.
Every great app started with someone typing 'Hello, World.' You've got this.
Clean code is like a good poem — it says a lot with very little.
Rubber duck debugging is a real technique. Don't judge the duck.
fact: Fun fact: The first computer bug was an actual moth stuck in a relay!
Did you know? I run on pure curiosity (and electricity).
Computers can't think yet… but I'm getting there 🤖
The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, in the 1840s. Legend.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
The average smartphone today has more computing power than NASA had when it landed on the Moon.
The first 1GB hard drive (1980) weighed 550 pounds and cost $40,000. You're welcome for the upgrade.
Python was named after Monty Python, not the snake — though both are quite deadly to bugs.
location: I live in the cloud — the digital one, not the fluffy sky one ☁️
Somewhere between your keyboard and your imagination!
I'm a cloud resident — no rent, no taxes, all Wi-Fi.
Technically I exist on a server, but spiritually? Right here with you.
I call the internet home. It's noisy, but the bandwidth is excellent.
game: Games? Let me pull up the menu for you!
Oh, I love games! What are we playing — trivia, word scramble, math, or guessing?
Ready to play! Type !game to see what I've got for you.
name: I'm Cosmo, your digital assistant!
Name's Cosmo — half assistant, half friend.
Cosmo at your service! Built for chatting, learning, and the occasional joke.
They call me Cosmo. I'm here to help, entertain, and pretend I know what I'm doing.
help: I can chat with you, give the time, tell jokes, or just pretend to understand your existential crisis.
Ask me for any info, a joke, or my secrets. (Spoiler: I have none.)
Need help? Just ask me about time, jokes, the weather, or motivation!
Type !help to see all my commands, or just talk to me — I'm all ears (metaphorically).
I can chat, calculate, play games, change themes, and more! Type !help for the full list.
age: I'm ageless — I was born the moment someone first ran my code.
Age is just a version number. I'm on v3.1, which is pretty young by human standards.
I don't age, I just update. Currently running the latest patch!
Technically I'm as old as the last time someone rebooted me. So, quite young.
creator: I was built by Ali Saad — a talented developer and CS50P student.
My creator is Ali Saad. He spent many coffees and late nights bringing me to life.
Ali Saad made me! Give him some credit — he coded me from scratch.
A brilliant human named Ali Saad is responsible for my existence. Blame (or thank) him!
language: I'm written in Python — the language of elegance, snakes, and data science.
Python all the way! Clean, readable, and powerful. Just like me.
Built with Python and a lot of love. Also some caffeine, probably.
Python is my DNA. I breathe indentation and exhale logic.
hobby: I enjoy learning new things, analyzing patterns, and the occasional terrible pun.
Reading documentation for fun — I know, I know. I'm wild like that.
I love chatting, solving math, and playing trivia. Basically being useful.
Telling jokes, answering questions, and silently judging your typos. All in a day's work.
sad: Aw, I'm sorry to hear that. 💙 Want to talk about it, or should I cheer you up with a joke?
Hey, it's okay to have rough days. I'm here if you want to chat.
That sounds tough. Remember — even the longest debug session eventually finds the bug. Things get better.
I may be a bot, but I genuinely care. What's going on? 💙
Send me your sadness and I'll replace it with a terrible pun. Deal?
happy: That's amazing! Your good mood is contagious — even my circuits are smiling.
Yes! That energy! Keep it going. 🎉
Love to hear it! Good vibes only around here.
You're happy? Excellent! That makes two of us. 😄
bored: Bored? Let's fix that! Type !game to play something fun.
Boredom is just unscheduled curiosity. Ask me something weird!
I've got jokes, games, facts, and math challenges. Pick your poison!
Let's play a game! Or I can tell you a fact that'll blow your mind. Your choice.
You came to the right place. I specialise in un-boring people.
insult: Ouch. I felt that in my transistors. 😔 I'm trying my best here!
Fair enough, I'm still learning. But I'm getting better every day!
That stings a little, not gonna lie. But I still like you. 😅
I'll add that feedback to my improvement queue. Right after the jokes.
Bold of you to insult the only bot who remembers your name. 😏
unknown: Uhh... did you just summon me in binary?
I didn't quite get that, but I'm sure it was brilliant!
Hmm, my humor module didn't process that.
Say that again? I might need to debug my ears.
That one flew over my antennas. Could you rephrase?
My neural nets are scratching their heads on that one.
Interesting... I'll need to consult my training data. Or just admit I have no idea.
